Hacker News Confidence

Less annoying hacker news. (What is this?)

This site scrapes Hacker News once every 30 minutes, then sorts according to this formula, with upvotes as positive signals and comments as negative ones. In my experience, the best articles to read on HN are the ones with a high upvote-to-discussion ratio. Mostly because controversial pieces tend to produce a disproportionate number of comments compared to upvotes.


  1. Attorney General Pam Bondi Out at DOJ 29 upvotes, 1 comments
  2. An Example of Statistical Investigation of the Text Eugene Onegin – Markov, 1913 [pdf] 22 upvotes, 1 comments
  3. Modern SQLite: Features You Didn't Know It Had 91 upvotes, 16 comments
  4. Lemonade by AMD: a fast and open source local LLM server using GPU and NPU 325 upvotes, 82 comments
  5. Google releases Gemma 4 open models 587 upvotes, 163 comments
  6. Email obfuscation: What works in 2026? 302 upvotes, 88 comments
  7. Emacs-libgterm: Terminal emulator for Emacs using libghostty-vt 71 upvotes, 16 comments
  8. Qwen3.6-Plus: Towards real world agents 295 upvotes, 100 comments
  9. JSON Canvas Spec 25 upvotes, 4 comments
  10. EmDash: A Fresh Take on CMS 39 upvotes, 9 comments
  11. LinkedIn is illegally searching your computer 1249 upvotes, 573 comments
  12. Inside Nepal's Fake Rescue Racket 206 upvotes, 82 comments
  13. Artemis II will use laser beams to live-stream 4K moon footage at 260 Mbps 210 upvotes, 87 comments
  14. Tailscale's New macOS Home 39 upvotes, 11 comments
  15. Sweden goes back to basics, swapping screens for books in the classroom 606 upvotes, 310 comments
  16. Hugo's New CSS Powers 10 upvotes, 1 comments
  17. Delve allegedly forked an open-source tool and sold it as its own 188 upvotes, 90 comments
  18. Gone (Almost) Phishin' 146 upvotes, 68 comments
  19. Significant Raise of Reports 230 upvotes, 115 comments
  20. Renewables reached nearly 50% of global electricity capacity last year 139 upvotes, 66 comments
  21. 'Backrooms' and the Rise of the Institutional Gothic 113 upvotes, 57 comments
  22. Bringing Clojure programming to Enterprise (2021) 168 upvotes, 96 comments
  23. IBM Announces Strategic Collaboration with Arm 237 upvotes, 151 comments
  24. Quadratic Micropass Type Inference 14 upvotes, 4 comments
  25. George Goble died recently – known for first dual-CPU-Unix and fast BBQ lighting 15 upvotes, 5 comments
  26. Ask HN: European Tech Alternatives? 14 upvotes, 5 comments
  27. Cursor 3 64 upvotes, 46 comments
  28. Reinventing the pull request 69 upvotes, 54 comments
  29. A forecast of the fair market value of SpaceX's businesses 82 upvotes, 114 comments
  30. Zep AI Is Hiring – Building the Agent Context Layer (YC W24) 0 upvotes, 0 comments
